subject: feat(modal): Phase 1 — migrate QuickGuide + InstallInstructionsModal to DaisyUI modal
body:
First of 10 phases from the DaisyUI component-class audit. This phase
migrates the two self-contained modal components to DaisyUI's `modal`
component classes, eliminating ~35 custom CSS class definitions.

Changes:

QuickGuide.jsx — replaced bespoke .quick-guide-overlay + .quick-guide-modal
with DaisyUI's `<div className="modal modal-open">` + `<div className="modal-box">`
+ `<div className="modal-backdrop">` structure. Close button is now
`btn btn-sm btn-circle btn-ghost` (absolutely positioned with Tailwind
utilities). Skip/Next buttons are `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` / `btn btn-primary
btn-sm` in a `modal-action` footer. Pagination dots converted from
.quick-guide-dot to inline Tailwind (w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-primary /
bg-base-300). Content text uses text-base-content / text-base-content/80
for the title/body hierarchy.

Modal-box width preserved via inline Tailwind (w-[min(420px,calc(100vw-32px))])
so the small-screen responsive cap still applies. Padding adjusted from
px-28-pt-32-pb-24 to the DaisyUI default with small overrides. Focus trap
and useEscapeKey hooks unchanged — they operate on the ref-held
container regardless of its class.

InstallInstructionsModal.jsx — same modal structure. The data-driven
numbered-step list is now a flex column with each step rendered as
<li class="flex items-start gap-3"> + a circular primary-tinted span for
the number. The optional warning note moves from .install-modal-note
to `<div role="alert" className="alert alert-warning alert-soft">` —
proper ARIA role AND DaisyUI alert component class. Benefits list keeps
its leading checkmarks as Unicode characters in the text (was a
::before pseudo-element before — simpler to put them in the JSX now
that the surrounding list is a flex column). Final "Got it" button is
`btn btn-primary btn-block` inside a modal-action wrapper.

styles.css — deleted two blocks (~130 lines total):
  - .quick-guide-overlay / .quick-guide-modal / .quick-guide-close /
    .quick-guide-content / .quick-guide-icon / .quick-guide-title /
    .quick-guide-body / .quick-guide-footer / .quick-guide-dots /
    .quick-guide-dot / .quick-guide-dot.active / .quick-guide-actions /
    .quick-guide-btn-primary / .quick-guide-btn-secondary + 3 keyframes
  - .install-modal-overlay / .install-modal-backdrop / .install-modal /
    .install-modal-header / .install-modal-steps / .install-modal-step /
    .install-modal-step-number / .install-modal-note /
    .install-modal-benefits / .install-modal-close

Each block replaced with a one-line comment pointing at the component
file so future contributors know why the class was removed.

Print override (@media print) — updated the "hide interactive chrome
when printing" rule from `.quick-guide-overlay, .quick-guide-modal` to
just `.modal` (DaisyUI's wrapper class, which now hosts both QuickGuide
and InstallInstructionsModal).

What's preserved:

- Focus trap via useFocusTrap — ref still threads through the modal-box
- Escape-to-close via useEscapeKey — unchanged
- Click-outside-to-close via onClick on .modal-backdrop — now a
  semantically-named DaisyUI class instead of custom .quick-guide-overlay
- Responsive width cap (min(420px, calc(100vw - 32px))) via inline
  Tailwind arbitrary value — matches the pre-migration visual
- Scroll lock via useScrollLock — unchanged (QuickGuide only)

Build + tests:
  - npm test: 21 passing, 0 failing, ~160 ms
  - npm run build: passes, 84 modules. CSS 160.06 → ~160 KB (tiny delta)
  - DaisyUI modal, modal-box, modal-backdrop, modal-action, modal-open
    classes all present in built CSS
  - All 4 QuickGuide step titles and all 3 install-instruction UI
    strings still present in built JS
  - All deleted custom classes confirmed GONE from built CSS

Tags: ui, daisyui, refactor, a11y, modal
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 1
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-component-migration
Semver: patch
